string-functions.label: String Functions (TRIM, UPPER, LOWER)
string-functions.summary: Clean and transform text data with string functions.

sketch-intro-string-funcs.title: Introduction to String Functions
sketch-intro-string-funcs.bodyMarkdown:
String functions in SQL help you clean and transform text data. Common functions include:

- **TRIM(text)**: Removes leading and trailing spaces from a string.
- **UPPER(text)**: Converts all letters in a string to uppercase.
- **LOWER(text)**: Converts all letters in a string to lowercase.

These are especially useful for standardizing data, making searches case-insensitive, or cleaning up user input.

sketch-examples-string-funcs.title: Examples Using the Customers Table
sketch-examples-string-funcs.bodyMarkdown:
Suppose you have the following row in the `customers` table:

| id | full_name      | email             |
|----|---------------|-------------------|
| 3  |  Ava Patel  | ava@example.com    |

- Using `TRIM(full_name)` will return `Ava Patel` (removes extra spaces).
- Using `UPPER(full_name)` will return `AVA PATEL`.
- Using `LOWER(email)` will return `ava@example.com` (already lowercase, but ensures consistency).

You can combine these functions as needed in your SELECT statements.

quiz-single-choice-trim.title: What does the TRIM function do?
quiz-single-choice-trim.prompt: Which of the following best describes the purpose of the TRIM function in SQL?
quiz-single-choice-trim.hint: Think about where extra spaces might appear in user input.

help.concept: The TRIM function is used to remove unwanted spaces from the start and end of a string, which is common in data entry.
help.hint_1: TRIM does not remove all spaces, only those at the edges.
help.hint_2: TRIM is useful for cleaning up leading and trailing spaces, not for changing the case or replacing characters.

options.a: Removes all spaces from a string
options.b: Removes spaces only at the beginning and end of a string

quiz-single-choice-upper.title: Using UPPER for Standardization
quiz-single-choice-upper.prompt: Why might you use the UPPER function when querying email addresses?
quiz-single-choice-upper.hint: Think about how text comparisons work in SQL.

help.concept: UPPER is used to convert all letters to uppercase, which helps standardize text for comparisons.
help.hint_1: UPPER does not remove spaces or extract parts of the email.
help.hint_2: Converting both sides of a comparison to uppercase ensures case differences don't affect the result.

options.c: To extract the domain from emails

quiz-drag-reorder-string-funcs.title: Order of String Cleanup Steps
quiz-drag-reorder-string-funcs.prompt: Arrange the steps to clean up a name field for consistent searching.
quiz-drag-reorder-string-funcs.hint: Think about what should be cleaned first.

help.concept: Cleaning text often involves removing extra spaces before changing the case, then performing comparisons.
help.hint_1: Start by removing spaces that might affect the result.
help.hint_2: After trimming, convert to lowercase for case-insensitive comparison.

quiz-fill-blank-choice-string-func.title: Choose the Correct Function
quiz-fill-blank-choice-string-func.prompt: Fill in the blank: To convert the `city` column to all uppercase letters in a SELECT statement, use _____.
quiz-fill-blank-choice-string-func.hint: Which function changes text to uppercase?

help.concept: the missing term is the SQL function for converting text to all capital letters.
help.hint_1: TRIM removes spaces, LOWER makes text lowercase.
help.hint_2: the missing term is used for uppercase conversion.

quiz-code-input-trim-upper.title: Clean Up Names for Display
quiz-code-input-trim-upper.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the `id` and a cleaned-up version of `full_name` from the `customers` table. The cleaned-up name should have no leading/trailing spaces and be in uppercase. Name the new column `clean_name`.
quiz-code-input-trim-upper.hint: You need to use both TRIM and UPPER together.

help.concept: You can nest string functions in SQL to first remove spaces and then change the case.
help.hint_1: Apply TRIM to full_name first, then wrap it with UPPER.
help.hint_2: The inner function should remove spaces, and the outer function should convert to uppercase.

quiz-code-input-trim-upper.starterCode:
SELECT id, _____(_____(full_name)) AS clean_name
FROM customers;
